story.iWant: multi-platform CLI binaries automatically built and published to GitHub Releases whenever a version tag is pushed
story.soThat: I can download and run Recipe CLI on my platform (Linux/macOS/Windows, amd64/arm64) without needing to install Go or build from source
story.tasks:
### Task 1: Create CHANGELOG.md (AC-8) - [ ] Create CHANGELOG.md file in repository root - [ ] Write initial changelog content with Keep a Changelog format - [ ] Document version 0.1.0 with all Epic 1-7 changes - [ ] Commit CHANGELOG.md to repository
### Task 2: Create GitHub Actions Workflow File (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3) - [ ] Create .github/workflows/ directory if not exists - [ ] Create release.yml workflow file - [ ] Configure workflow trigger (push to tags matching v*) - [ ] Configure build matrix (3 OS × 2 arch = 6 binaries) - [ ] Add build steps (checkout, setup Go, build, generate checksums, upload) - [ ] Commit workflow file
### Task 3: Test Workflow with Test Tag (AC-1, AC-2, AC-3, AC-6) - [ ] Create test tag v0.0.1 - [ ] Monitor workflow execution in GitHub Actions - [ ] Verify 6 parallel build jobs execute successfully - [ ] Verify GitHub Release created with 12 assets (6 binaries + 6 checksums) - [ ] Measure workflow duration (target: <10 minutes) - [ ] Test binary downloads and checksum verification - [ ] Cleanup test tags
### Task 4: Update README.md with Installation Instructions (AC-7) - [ ] Add Installation section to README.md - [ ] Include platform-specific instructions (Linux, macOS, Windows) - [ ] Document checksum verification process - [ ] Include build from source instructions - [ ] Commit README update
### Task 5: Document Versioning Strategy in CHANGELOG.md (AC-5) - [ ] Add versioning strategy section to CHANGELOG.md - [ ] Explain semantic versioning (MAJOR.MINOR.PATCH) - [ ] Document release process - [ ] Commit CHANGELOG update
### Task 6: Add Release Notes Template (AC-4) - [ ] Update workflow to include release body template - [ ] Include installation instructions in release notes - [ ] Add checksum verification instructions - [ ] Add CHANGELOG.md link - [ ] Commit workflow update
### Task 7: Test Trigger Behavior (AC-1) - [ ] Test non-version tag (should NOT trigger) - [ ] Test branch push (should NOT trigger) - [ ] Test version tag (should trigger) - [ ] Cleanup test tags and releases
### Task 8: Binary Validation Testing (AC-2) - [ ] Download all 6 binaries from GitHub Release - [ ] Test Linux amd64 binary (--version, --help, convert) - [ ] Test macOS arm64 binary - [ ] Test Windows amd64 binary - [ ] Verify binary sizes reasonable (<50MB each)
### Task 9: Prepare First Production Release (AC-5, AC-8) - [ ] Review CHANGELOG.md for completeness - [ ] Finalize version number (v0.1.0 recommended) - [ ] Update CHANGELOG.md for v0.1.0 - [ ] Commit final CHANGELOG - [ ] Create production tag v0.1.0 - [ ] Monitor release workflow - [ ] Verify GitHub Release created with all assets
### Task 10: Update sprint-status.yaml - [ ] Mark story 7-6 as "drafted" - [ ] Commit sprint status update

story-context.acceptanceCriteria:
### AC-1: GitHub Actions Workflow Triggers on Version Tag Push - Workflow file exists at .github/workflows/release.yml - Trigger configuration: on push to tags matching 'v*' - Tag pattern matching: v1.0.0, v0.1.0, etc. - Does NOT trigger on non-version tags or branch pushes - Workflow run visible in GitHub Actions within 30 seconds
### AC-2: Workflow Builds 6 CLI Binaries - Build matrix: 3 OS (linux, darwin, windows) × 2 arch (amd64, arm64) = 6 binaries - Binaries: recipe-linux-amd64, recipe-linux-arm64, recipe-darwin-amd64, recipe-darwin-arm64, recipe-windows-amd64.exe, recipe-windows-arm64.exe - Build command uses GOOS/GOARCH for cross-compilation - Build flags: -ldflags="-s -w" strips debug symbols - 6 jobs run in parallel (total build time <10 minutes)
### AC-3: Binaries are Uploaded to GitHub Release - GitHub Release created automatically with tag name - All 6 binaries attached as downloadable assets - Asset names match binary names - Download URLs functional - Release visible at https://github.com/{user}/recipe/releases/tag/{tag}
### AC-4: Release Includes CHANGELOG Excerpt - Release description includes changes for this version - Extracted from CHANGELOG.md or auto-generated - Follows Keep a Changelog standard - Full changelog link provided
### AC-5: Semantic Versioning Followed - Tag format: vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH (e.g., v1.0.0) - Versioning rules: MAJOR (breaking), MINOR (features), PATCH (fixes) - CHANGELOG.md documents strategy with semver reference - Initial version: v0.1.0 (beta) or v1.0.0 (stable)
### AC-6: Build Completes in <10 Minutes - Total duration <10 minutes from workflow start to completion - Workflow timeout set to 15 minutes (safety) - Parallel matrix jobs (not sequential) - Measured and documented in workflow logs
### AC-7: README.md Updated with Installation Instructions - Installation section exists with platform-specific instructions - Linux/macOS: curl download, chmod, move to PATH - Windows: download link, PATH setup - Latest release link provided - Build from source instructions included
### AC-8: CHANGELOG.md Maintained - CHANGELOG.md exists in repository root - Follows Keep a Changelog format - Version entries with semantic versioning - Sections: Added, Changed, Fixed, Removed - Unreleased section for in-progress changes - Update process documented

artifact.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification
artifact.snippet: AC-1 to AC-8 define complete workflow requirements: GitHub Actions workflow triggers on v* tags, builds 6 CLI binaries (Linux/macOS/Windows × amd64/arm64), uploads to GitHub Release with CHANGELOG excerpt, follows semantic versioning (vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH), completes in <10 minutes, README.md includes installation instructions, CHANGELOG.md maintained with Keep a Changelog format.

artifact.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification
artifact.section: Data Models and Contracts - GitHub Releases Workflow
artifact.snippet: Workflow configuration schema defines build matrix (os: [linux, darwin, windows], arch: [amd64, arm64]), build command (GOOS/GOARCH cross-compilation with ldflags="-s -w"), and upload action (softprops/action-gh-release@v1). Semantic version tag format: vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH. CHANGELOG.md follows Keep a Changelog standard.

artifact.title: Epic 7 Technical Specification
artifact.section: Workflows and Sequencing - Workflow 2: GitHub Release Creation
artifact.snippet: Developer creates tag (git tag v1.0.0, git push origin v1.0.0) → GitHub Actions triggers release.yml → Build matrix executes 6 parallel jobs → Upload binaries to release → Release published at https://github.com/{user}/recipe/releases/tag/v1.0.0. Total time: 5-10 minutes.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: Requirement: Binaries available for Linux/macOS/Windows (amd64/arm64). Distribution via GitHub Releases (manual download). Validation: Binaries downloadable from GitHub Releases page.

artifact.title: Product Requirements Document
artifact.snippet: Semantic versioning required: vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH. CHANGELOG.md maintained following Keep a Changelog format. Version tags trigger automated release builds.

artifact.title: Story 7-5: Cloudflare Pages Deployment
artifact.snippet: Story 7-5 established GitHub Actions CI/CD pattern for web deployment. Shared patterns with 7-6: GitHub Actions workflow, trigger mechanism, Go toolchain (setup-go@v5 with Go 1.24), build optimization (-ldflags="-s -w"), timeout safety. Both use .github/workflows/{name}.yml structure.

artifact.reason: Existing multi-platform build target demonstrates GOOS/GOARCH cross-compilation pattern. Currently builds 4 platforms (linux-amd64, darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64). Story 7-6 needs to add linux-arm64 and windows-arm64 to match 6-binary requirement.
Entry point for CLI binary builds. This file will be compiled for each target platform (GOOS/GOARCH combinations) in GitHub Actions workflow.
Cobra CLI root command definition. Provides --version flag output that will be tested for binary validation in AC-2.
Primary CLI command for file conversion. Binaries must include this functionality - will be tested during binary validation (Task 8).
Defines Go version requirement (1.25.1). GitHub Actions workflow must use compatible Go version (1.24+ as specified in tech spec).
Must be updated with installation instructions per AC-7. Will include download URLs, platform-specific instructions, and checksum verification process.

constraint.name: Cross-Compilation Requirements
constraint.description: GitHub Actions workflow must build 6 CLI binaries using Go cross-compilation: 3 OS (linux, darwin, windows) × 2 architectures (amd64, arm64). Build command pattern: GOOS={os} GOARCH={arch} go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o recipe-{os}-{arch} cmd/cli/main.go

constraint.name: Binary Size Optimization
constraint.description: All binaries must be built with ldflags="-s -w" to strip debug symbols and reduce file size by ~30%. Target: each binary <50MB. Current Makefile demonstrates this pattern.

constraint.name: GitHub Actions Free Tier Limits
constraint.description: Public repositories have 2,000 minutes/month of GitHub Actions. Workflow must complete in <10 minutes to allow ~200 releases/month (realistic: 10-20 releases/month). Use ubuntu-latest runner (free, unlimited minutes for public repos).

constraint.name: Tag Pattern Matching
constraint.description: Workflow trigger MUST use pattern 'v*' to match semantic version tags (v1.0.0, v0.1.0, etc.) and avoid triggering on non-version tags. Pattern: on.push.tags: ['v*']

constraint.name: Semantic Versioning Adherence
constraint.description: All version tags must follow vMAJOR.MINOR.PATCH format. MAJOR: breaking changes, MINOR: new features (backward compatible), PATCH: bug fixes. Initial release recommended: v0.1.0 (beta status, allows iteration).

constraint.name: Keep a Changelog Format
constraint.description: CHANGELOG.md must follow Keep a Changelog standard (https://keepachangelog.com/). Sections: Added, Changed, Fixed, Removed, Deprecated, Security. Version entries in reverse chronological order with YYYY-MM-DD dates.

constraint.name: GitHub Token Permissions
constraint.description: Workflow uses auto-provided GITHUB_TOKEN with sufficient permissions for release creation and asset uploads. No manual secret configuration required (unlike Cloudflare deployment in Story 7-5).

constraint.name: Epic 3 CLI Completion Required
constraint.description: Stories 3-1 through 3-6 must be complete before Story 7-6 implementation. CLI must be functional with working --version, --help, and convert commands. Test manual build before implementing workflow.

constraint.name: Zero-Cost Infrastructure
constraint.description: GitHub Releases free tier: unlimited releases, 2GB per release file size limit. Combined with GitHub Actions free tier, entire deployment infrastructure costs $0/month. No paid upgrades required for MVP.

constraint.name: Parallel Build Execution
constraint.description: Build matrix jobs MUST run in parallel (not sequential) to meet <10 minute requirement. Total time = slowest job + upload time, not sum of all jobs. GitHub Actions default behavior is parallel for matrix jobs.

interface.name: GitHub Actions Workflow - release.yml
interface.signature: on: push: tags: ['v*'] jobs: build: runs-on: ubuntu-latest timeout-minutes: 15 strategy: matrix: os: [linux, darwin, windows] arch: [amd64, arm64] steps: - uses: actions/checkout@v4 - uses: actions/setup-go@v5 with: go-version: '1.24' - name: Build run: GOOS=${{ matrix.os }} GOARCH=${{ matrix.arch }} go build -ldflags="-s -w" -o recipe-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}${{ matrix.os == 'windows' && '.exe' || '' }} cmd/cli/main.go - name: Generate checksums run: sha256sum recipe-* > recipe-${{ matrix.os }}-${{ matrix.arch }}.sha256 - uses: softprops/action-gh-release@v1 with: files: recipe-* env: GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.GITHUB_TOKEN }}

interface.name: CHANGELOG.md Format
interface.signature:
# Changelog
All notable changes to Recipe will be documented in this file.
The format is based on [Keep a Changelog](https://keepachangelog.com/), and this project adheres to [Semantic Versioning](https://semver.org/).
## [Unreleased] ### Added ### Changed ### Fixed
## [0.1.0] - YYYY-MM-DD ### Added - Feature descriptions

interface.name: README.md Installation Section
interface.signature:
## Installation
### Download Pre-Built Binaries
**Latest Release:** [Download from GitHub Releases](https://github.com/{user}/recipe/releases/latest)
#### Linux / macOS ```bash curl -LO https://github.com/{user}/recipe/releases/latest/download/recipe-{os}-{arch} chmod +x recipe-{os}-{arch} sudo mv recipe-{os}-{arch} /usr/local/bin/recipe recipe --version ```
#### Windows Download from GitHub Releases page, add to PATH, run recipe.exe --version
### Verify Download Integrity ```bash sha256sum -c recipe-{os}-{arch}.sha256 ```

interface.name: GitHub Release API (via softprops/action-gh-release@v1)
Semantic Version Tag Format
interface.signature:
Tag pattern: v{MAJOR}.{MINOR}.{PATCH} Examples: v1.0.0, v0.1.0, v2.5.3
Rules: - MAJOR: Breaking changes (format incompatibility, API changes) - MINOR: New features (backward compatible) - PATCH: Bug fixes (no new features)
Initial version recommendation: v0.1.0 (beta/experimental)

interface.name: Binary Naming Convention
interface.signature:
Pattern: recipe-{os}-{arch}[.exe]
Examples: - recipe-linux-amd64 - recipe-linux-arm64 - recipe-darwin-amd64 - recipe-darwin-arm64 - recipe-windows-amd64.exe - recipe-windows-arm64.exe
Checksum files: {binary-name}.sha256

tests.standards: Epic 7 testing focuses on infrastructure validation (deployment pipelines) and documentation quality. No unit tests required - testing is primarily manual verification and CI/CD monitoring. Key standards: (1) CI/CD Pipeline Testing via GitHub Actions integration tests, (2) Binary Validation through manual download and execution testing on all 6 platforms, (3) Performance Validation measuring deployment speed (<10 minutes) and build success rate, (4) Security Validation through workflow log audits (zero secret exposure), checksum verification (100% match), and HTTPS enforcement. Definition of Done requires: All 8 ACs verified PASS, GitHub Release created with 6 binaries downloadable, Performance NFRs met (build <10 min), Security NFRs met (secrets secure, checksums valid), Zero P0/P1 bugs outstanding.

idea.description: Workflow Trigger Test: Create test tag v0.0.1, push to GitHub, verify workflow run appears in Actions tab within 30 seconds. Create non-version tag "test-tag", verify workflow does NOT trigger. Push to main branch, verify release workflow does NOT trigger.

Build Matrix Test: Monitor GitHub Actions logs for 6 parallel jobs (linux-amd64, linux-arm64, darwin-amd64, darwin-arm64, windows-amd64, windows-arm64). Verify each job completes successfully with green checkmark. Check build logs for GOOS/GOARCH environment variables set correctly.

Binary Upload Test: Navigate to Releases page, verify 6 binaries + 6 checksum files attached (12 assets total). Download one binary (e.g., recipe-linux-amd64), verify file downloads successfully. Check download URL format matches expected pattern.
Release Notes Test: View release page, verify description not empty. If CHANGELOG.md exists, verify excerpt matches version entry. Verify "Full changelog" link points to CHANGELOG.md in repository.

Semantic Versioning Test: Review git tags (git tag -l), verify all tags follow vX.Y.Z pattern. Review CHANGELOG.md, verify versioning strategy documented with semver reference. Verify tag pattern in workflow matches 'v*'.

Performance Test: Create test tag, monitor workflow execution time from start to completion. Verify duration <10 minutes (target met). Check workflow timeout configuration (should be 15 minutes as safety net). Document actual timing in workflow logs.

README Installation Test: Read README.md Installation section, verify platform-specific instructions present for Linux/macOS/Windows. Follow Linux instructions manually: curl download, chmod, verify --version works. Test download URL (https://github.com/{user}/recipe/releases/latest/download/recipe-linux-amd64) is functional.

CHANGELOG Format Test: Verify CHANGELOG.md exists in repository root. Check format matches Keep a Changelog standard (header, sections: Added/Changed/Fixed/Removed, version entries with dates). Verify Unreleased section present. Verify semver reference link included.

Binary Validation Test (All Platforms): Download all 6 binaries from GitHub Release. Test Linux amd64: chmod +x recipe-linux-amd64, ./recipe-linux-amd64 --version (expect version output), ./recipe-linux-amd64 --help (expect help text). Test macOS arm64 on Apple Silicon device. Test Windows amd64: recipe-windows-amd64.exe --version. Verify binary sizes reasonable (<50MB each).

Checksum Verification Test: Download binary (recipe-linux-amd64) and checksum file (recipe-linux-amd64.sha256). Run: sha256sum -c recipe-linux-amd64.sha256. Verify output: "recipe-linux-amd64: OK". Repeat for at least 2 other platforms to ensure checksums generated correctly.

Workflow Failure Recovery Test: Introduce syntax error in cmd/cli/main.go, create test tag, push. Verify workflow fails gracefully with clear error message. Verify GitHub commit shows red X. Verify email notification sent (if enabled). Fix error, create new tag, verify workflow succeeds.

End-to-End Release Test: Simulate full release process: Update CHANGELOG.md with v0.1.0 entry, commit, create tag (git tag v0.1.0), push tag (git push origin v0.1.0). Monitor GitHub Actions workflow, verify 6 binaries built and uploaded. Download binary, verify conversion works (recipe convert testdata/np3/sample.np3 output.xmp). Verify release page has correct version, changelog excerpt, and download links.
